headline: Mandatory death penalty for drug trafficking repealed but 780 await resentencing
context: Following the 2023 abolition of mandatory death for drug trafficking, 780 inmates convicted under the Dangerous Drugs Act await resentencing hearings.

cards.big: 780 drug offenders on death row await resentencing 2 years after reform
cards.sub: Court backlog means most will wait until 2027 or later for hearings

cards.big: DDA 1952 Section 39B previously imposed mandatory death for trafficking above threshold
cards.sub: New sentencing guidelines allow courts to impose 30-40 years or life imprisonment

cards.big: 75% of DDA death row inmates were convicted as drug mules, not kingpins
cards.sub: Mandatory sentencing failed to distinguish between roles in drug trade

cards.big: Families of 780 inmates formed support network across 12 states in 2024
cards.sub: Average death row inmate has spent 9.3 years awaiting execution or reform

cards.big: Reform without resources for resentencing extends the punishment it claims to end
Court of Appeal assigned 4 additional judges to handle resentencing cases in 2025
